verdict_reasoning: LYN is a Src-family tyrosine kinase that is N-terminally myristoylated and palmitoylated, anchoring it to the cytoplasmic (inner) leaflet of the plasma membrane — making it canonically inner-leaflet-anchored and not directly accessible from the extracellular face. However, multiple lines of evidence support contextual outer-leaflet or surface-accessible biology: (1) Cancer ecto-kinase reports describe LYN on the outer surface of tumor cells, consistent with the documented class of cancer-cell-restricted ecto-display of nominally intracellular Src-family kinases. (2) LYN participates in mast cell degranulation and B-cell receptor signaling, contexts where activation-induced redistribution and surface-complex association occur. (3) Several surface-proteomics datasets have detected Src-family kinases including LYN in cell-surface fractions. The canonical localization is inner-leaflet, but the cancer ecto-pool and activation-associated surface display qualify this as contextual/cell_state_induced rather than a hard no.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Evidence for LYN outer-leaflet display in cancer or activated immune cells is suggestive but not as robustly characterized as for some other Src-family ecto-kinases; extent and washability of the surface pool is uncertain.
